questions.subject: Physics
questions.q: A scooter, which together with its rider weighs $150 \text{ kg}$, is travelling at $36 \text{ km/hr}$. Determine the horizontal force on the scooter needed to successfully navigate a turn of radius $30 \text{ m}$.
questions.solution:
Mass of the scooter and rider, $m = 150 \text{ kg}$

Velocity of the scooter, $v = 36 \text{ km/hr} = 36 \times \dfrac{5}{18} \text{ m/s} = 10 \text{ m/s}$

Radius of the turn, $r = 30 \text{ m}$

The horizontal force required to navigate the turn is the centripetal force, which is given by:

$F = \dfrac{mv^2}{r}$

Substituting the given values:

$F = \dfrac{150 \times (10)^2}{30}$

$F = \dfrac{150 \times 100}{30} = 500 \text{ N}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.q: A park has a radius of $10 \text{ m}$. Determine the proper angle of banking if a vehicle travels around it at an average speed of $18 \text{ km/hr}$.
questions.solution:
Given radius of the park, $r = 10 \text{ m}$

Speed of the vehicle, $v = 18 \text{ km/hr} = 18 \times \dfrac{5}{18} \text{ m/s} = 5 \text{ m/s}$

The proper angle of banking $\theta$ is given by the formula:

$\tan \theta = \dfrac{v^2}{rg}$

Taking $g = 10 \text{ m/s}^2$ and substituting the values:

$\tan \theta = \dfrac{5^2}{10 \times 10}$

$\tan \theta = \dfrac{25}{100} = \dfrac{1}{4}$

$\theta = \tan^{-1}\left(\dfrac{1}{4}\right)$
